CANADA_SPOUSE_OWP_INFO.title: Spouse Open Work Permit Eligibility
description: Under the new rules implemented in January 2025, a student's spouse or common-law partner is only eligible for an Open Work Permit if the student is enrolled in one of the following programs:

list: **Masterâ€™s or Doctoral Programs:** The program must be at least 16 months in duration. (Short Master's programs under 16 months no longer qualify the spouse for a work permit).
**Professional Degree Programs:** Spouses of students in specific professional bachelorâ€™s programs are still eligible.

description: These typically include:

list: Medicine (MD)
Law (LLB, JD, BCL)
Pharmacy (PharmD, BSc)
Veterinary Medicine (DVM)
Dentistry (DDS, DMD)
Optometry (OD)
Nursing (BScN - in some specific provincial frameworks)

description: **Important:** If the student is studying a standard Undergraduate Bachelorâ€™s degree (not in the professional list above) or a College Diploma, their spouse is **not eligible for an Open Work Permit**. They can still apply for a Visitor Visa, but they will not be allowed to work.

CANADA_CHILDREN_DEPENDENT_INFO.title: Bringing Dependent Children to Canada
description: Filipino students can still bring their dependent children regardless of the program level (Diploma, Degree, or Master's).

list: **Minor Children (under 18/19):** They can accompany the student and are generally eligible to study at a Canadian primary or secondary school without needing their own separate Study Permit (though having one is often recommended for smoother processing).
**Dependent Children (under 22):** Must be unmarried and financially dependent on the parents.

CANADA_ACADEMIC_REGRESSION_WARNING.title: Visa Strategy: [highestEducation] Holders (Canada)
description: If a student already holds a [highestEducation] from the Philippines and is applying for a [programCategory] in Canada, it is a major red flag for **"Academic Regression."**
**How to Overcome the Red Flag**
Unlike Australia, which strictly requires academic "upgrading," Canada is more flexible if the student can show **Career Logic**.

list: **Avoid General Programs:** Do not apply for a "General Business Diploma" if the student already has a Business degree. This is a 100% "Purpose of Visit" refusal.
**Focus on Specialization:** The course must be a Post-Graduate (PG) Diploma or a highly specialized Certificate (e.g., BS Nursing graduate taking a PG Diploma in Gerontology).

**Logical Progression:** The SOP must explain that the student isn't "re-studying" but is "specializing" to gain specific skills or Canadian work experience (Co-op) that will increase their salary back in the Philippines.

CANADA_BACHELOR_TO_BACHELOR_WARNING.title: Visa Strategy: Second Bachelorâ€™s Degree (Canada)
description: While a second degree is legally allowed, the Visa Officer will question why the student isn't moving up to a Master's or a Post-Graduate (PG) program.
**How to Handle the "Horizontal Movement" Red Flag**
To get an approval, the student must prove that the second degree provides a completely different skill set or is a mandatory requirement for their career goal.

list: **The "Field Switcher" Logic:** If a student has a Bachelor of Arts but now wants a Bachelor of Computer Science, this is acceptable. It shows a clear career pivot that requires a full foundation.

**The "Professional Licensing" Logic:** Some professions in Canada (like Engineering or Nursing) may require a Canadian degree for licensing. If the student can prove their Philippine degree isn't recognized for the specific job they want, the second degree is justified.
**Avoid "Same Field" Degrees:** If they have a BS in Business from the Philippines and apply for a Bachelor of Commerce in Canada, it will likely be refused for "Lack of Academic Progression."

CANADA_PGWP_DIPLOMA_INFO.title: Canada: Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP)
description: The [PGWP](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work/after-graduation/about.html) allows students to stay and work in Canada after graduation. For applications submitted on or after November 1, 2024, new eligibility 'filters' apply to all non-degree programs.
**1. The 'Field of Study' Filter (The Big Change)**

If the student is attending a College or a Non-University DLI for a Diploma, Certificate, Graduate Certificate, or Graduate Diploma, they are only eligible for a PGWP if their program falls under one of the 5 Priority Sectors:

list: Healthcare
STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Math)
Skilled Trades (Construction, Plumbing, etc.)
Transport
Agriculture & Agri-food

description: **2. Mandatory Language Requirement**
Every student applying for a PGWP in 2025 must provide a valid English test score (IELTS General, PTE Core, or CELPIP).

list: **University (Graduate Diploma/Cert):** Minimum CLB 7 (IELTS 6.0 in all bands).
**College (Diploma/Cert):** Minimum CLB 5 (IELTS 5.0 equivalent).

description: **3. PGWP Duration (How long can they stay?)**

list: **Diplomas/Certificates (2+ years):** 3-year PGWP (provided the field is eligible).
**Diplomas/Certificates (8 months â€“ 2 years):** Matches the length of the study program (e.g., a 1-year program gets a 1-year PGWP).

description: **The "CIP Code" Check**

Before a student pays their tuition to a DLI, you must check the programâ€™s CIP Code (Classification of Instructional Programs) on the [Official IRCC Eligibility List](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work/after-graduation/eligibility/field-of-study/currently-eligible.html). If the code is not on the list, they will not be able to work in Canada after they graduate.
**Consultant Tip:** Always double-check the school's specific program website to confirm if the CIP code is eligible before lodging.

CANADA_PGWP_DEGREE_INFO.title: Canada: Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP)
description: The [PGWP](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work/after-graduation/about.html) allows students to stay and work in Canada after graduation.
**1. The "Field of Study" Exemption (The Major Advantage)**
As of March 2025, students graduating with a [programCategory] (from either a University or a College) are exempt from field-of-study restrictions.

list: **The Rule:** You can study any subject (e.g., Business, Arts, Music, Psychology) and still be eligible for a PGWP.
**Note:** This exemption applies as long as the degree is recognized by the province and the institution is PGWP-eligible.

description: **2. Mandatory Language Requirement**
Every student applying for a PGWP in 2025 must provide a valid English test score (IELTS General, PTE Core, or CELPIP).

list: **Degree Graduates:** Minimum CLB 7 (IELTS General 6.0 in all bands).
**Important:** This is a higher requirement than for diploma graduates. Even if you were not required to take a test for your visa, you will need this score to get your work permit.

description: **3. PGWP Duration (How long can they stay?)**
[BACHELOR_PGWP_DURATION]
[PROFESSIONAL_PGWP_DURATION]
**The "Degree Status" Check**

Before a student pays their tuition, confirm that the program is officially classified as a Degree. In Canada, some colleges offer "Degrees" that are academically equivalent to University degrees. These are highly recommended because they bypass the strict 2025 CIP Code filters.

**Consultant Tip:** Because Bachelorâ€™s degrees are exempt from field-of-study restrictions, they are currently the lowest-risk option for students who want to study "non-priority" subjects like Business or Liberal Arts while still securing their future work rights in Canada.

CANADA_PGWP_MASTERS_PHD_INFO.title: Canada: PGWP for Masters & PhD Graduates
description: Graduates of Master and Doctorate programs keep the most generous PGWP pathway in 2025. Use this modal to set the right expectations before the student pays their tuition deposit.
**Why this pathway is safer:**

list: **No Field Restrictions:** Masterâ€™s and PhD degrees are automatically exempt from the PGWP priority field list, so the student can study any specialization and still qualify.
**Immigration Points:** A Canadian graduate degree provides the highest education score inside Express Entry and most Provincial Nominee Programs.
**Spouse Advantage:** These programs are among the few categories that still allow the partner to apply for an open work permit in 2025.

description: **What the student must prepare:**

list: **Language Test:** A CLB 7 (IELTS General 6.0 in all bands) is mandatory when they later apply for the PGWP or PR, even if the school waived the test for admission.
**Financial Readiness:** Tuition deposits are typically higher for graduate programs and must be paid before the school issues a Provincial Attestation Letter (PAL).

description: **PGWP Duration**
[MASTERS_PGWP_DURATION]
[PHD_PGWP_DURATION]
[SPOUSAL_OPEN_WORK_PERMIT_INFO]

Encourage the student to finalize their Statement of Purpose now while motivation is high. A well-written LOE that highlights career value and family ties will be reused for the visa, PGWP, and future PR steps.

CANADA_PGWP_MASTERS_INFO.title: Canada: Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP)
description: The [PGWP](https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work/after-graduation/about.html) allows students to stay and work in Canada after graduation. In 2025, [program category] graduates enjoy the most flexible rules and significant competitive advantages.
**1. The "Field of Study" Exemption**
Graduates of [program category] programs (from both Universities and select Colleges) are completely exempt from field-of-study restrictions.

list: **The Rule:** You can study any subject (MBA, Fine Arts, Philosophy, etc.) and you will still be eligible for a PGWP.
**Note:** Unlike diplomas, you do not need to check for a specific priority CIP code to qualify for the work permit.

description: **2. Mandatory Language Requirement**
Every student applying for a PGWP in 2025 must provide a valid English test score (IELTS General, PTE Core, or CELPIP).

list: **Requirement:** Minimum CLB 7 (IELTS General 6.0 in all bands).
**Validity:** Your test results must be less than 2 years old at the time of your PGWP application.

description: **3. PGWP Duration (The 3-Year Guarantee)**
As of 2024/2025, Masterâ€™s degrees have a unique duration advantage:
[MASTERS_PGWP_DURATION]
[PHD_PGWP_DURATION]
[SPOUSAL_OPEN_WORK_PERMIT_INFO]

Before a student chooses a 2-year Diploma, remind them that a 1-year Master's is now a better investment. It bypasses the "Priority Field" check, guarantees a 3-year work permit, and provides a much higher "score" for future Permanent Residency (PR) applications.

**Consultant Tip:** In 2025, a Master degree is the safest way to ensure a studentâ€™s spouse can work in Canada. Always check the program length on the schoolâ€™s website; if the Master's is only 12 months, the spouse may not be eligible for a work permit under the new 16-month rule.
